title: pundit_reset! does not clear @_pundit_policy_authorized flag consistently across Warden-based re-auth
body: When pundit_user changes mid-request (e.g. after Devise sign_in), pundit_reset! clears @pundit and the policy caches, but if verify_authorized already ran once it's easy to end up authorizing against the old cached policy object before pundit_reset! is called. Could the docs in Authorization be clearer about ordering?

title: PolicyFinder#find raises confusing error for anonymous Struct-based records
body: When passing a plain Struct.new instance (no model_name, no policy_class) through PolicyFinder#find, find_class_name falls through to subject.class, producing a policy name based on the anonymous struct's class, which is nil-like and hard to debug. Would be nice to raise a clearer NotDefinedError message pointing at the object itself.

title: Deprecation warning for `include Pundit` prints on every process boot, not just once
body: Pundit.included prints a WARNING heredoc with caller_locations every time a class includes the bare Pundit module. In apps with several controllers still on the old API this spams the log on every reload in development. Could this be de-duped or gated behind ActiveSupport::Deprecation instead of plain warn?

title: CacheStore::LegacyStore silently swallows non-Hash-like cache objects
body: Pundit::Authorization#pundit builds a LegacyStore.new(policies) where policies is just a plain Hash memoized on @_pundit_policies. If someone overrides #policies to return something that doesn't respond to []/[]=, LegacyStore blows up with a NoMethodError deep in Context#policy instead of a friendly Pundit error.

title: Document generator output for `rails g pundit:policy`
body: lib/generators/pundit/policy/policy_generator.rb generates a policy file but the README/inline docs don't show what the generated Scope class looks like by default, which confuses newcomers wiring up policy_scope for the first time.
